purpose: Declares the document layers that exist in this repo, their mutability and authority, and what happens when a document cannot be classified. Prevents ungoverned docs becoming de-facto truth.

layers.purpose: Timeless prose elaborating principles. May not contradict constitution or contracts.
Per-domain timeless law. May not contradict constitution or contracts.
Actual state of the running system (DB schemas, deployed configs, feature flags, API contracts). Lives in code and infra.
A time-boxed execution unit. Kit does not own plan format or location; projects decide.
layers.lifetime: bounded; closed and archived or deleted
layers.rename_rules: Projects own the format and location of plans. This layer exists so plans are not mistaken for doctrine.

layers.purpose: Regenerable fast-orientation summary. Never authoritative; only a cached read of truth.
layers.lifetime: regenerate on runtime-truth change or session close

layers.purpose: Tool-facing projection of repo law (e.g., CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md). Never authoritative.
layers.lifetime: lives as long as the tool is used

layers.purpose: Inline explanation attached to code. Follows the code's lifetime.
Documents produced by tools (API docs, changelogs). Regeneration policy owned by the generating tool.
Vendored third-party docs, licences, upstream READMEs. Treated as read-only projection of upstream truth.

on_new_doc: Classify into exactly one layer before committing
If the layer is unclear, halt and ask the human
Place the doc in a location matching the layer's location_pattern
on_ambiguous_layer: Propose a classification with confidence label; human resolves
